description_long: This object is a weathered stone Celtic cross monument, commonly found in cemeteries or parks as a memorial or historical marker. The cross features a circular ring connecting the arms, with four circular cutouts at the intersections, and is mounted on a rectangular plinth base. The surface is mottled with patches of lichen and discoloration, indicating significant age and exposure to the elements. The front face of the cross is adorned with intricate Celtic knotwork and patterns, while the rear and sides are plainer, showing more of the stone's natural texture. The tip of the cross is squared off, and the base is solid and slightly chipped, suggesting it is firmly anchored to the ground. The overall appearance is robust, with a rough, hard stone finish and a substantial, immovable presence.
description: A weathered stone Celtic cross monument with intricate knotwork on the front and a solid, lichen-covered base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the cross and the pointed tip, with visible weathering and discoloration.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full Celtic cross with detailed knotwork, circular cutouts, and a rectangular plinth base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the cross's thickness and the rough stone texture.
description_view_3: Rear view of the cross, showing less ornamentation and more of the stone's natural surface, with the base clearly visible.
attribute_ornamentation: Intricate Celtic knotwork and patterns carved on the front face of the cross, with circular cutouts at the intersections of the arms and ring.
attribute_base_design: The base is a rectangular plinth, solid and slightly chipped, with a rough stone texture and patches of lichen. It is unadorned and serves as a sturdy foundation.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is squared off and flat, with no additional decoration or unique features, maintaining the same rough stone finish as the rest of the cross.
